name: Ratatouille Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 small eggplant, pared and cut into 1/4 inch slices
1 large yellow squash (about 8 ounces), cut into 1/4 inch diagonal slices
1 medium zucchini, cut into 1/4 inch diagonal slices
1 medium yellow onion, very thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F. Spray a nonstick baking sheet and a 1-quart casserole dish with nonstick cooking spray.
2. In a medium bowl, combine eggplant and 1 teaspoon of oil; toss to coat.
3. Arrange eggplant slices in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet. Repeat with squash, then zucchini, tossing each with 1 teaspoon of oil and arranging separately in a single layer on the baking sheet. Bake for 12 minutes, then turn vegetables and bake an additional 8 minutes.
4. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F. In a large nonstick skillet, heat remaining 1 teaspoon oil over medium heat. Add onion, red pepper, and garlic; sauté for 5 minutes until onion is soft. Cook covered, shaking pan occasionally, for another 5 minutes.
5. In a small bowl, combine tomato paste with 1/2 cup water; set aside. In another small bowl, combine parsley, basil, thyme, salt, and pepper.
6. In the prepared casserole dish, arrange half of the baked eggplant slices and sprinkle with 1 teaspoon herb mixture. Continue layering with half of each sliced tomato, baked squash, onion mixture, and baked zucchini, sprinkling 1 teaspoon herb mixture between each layer. Repeat, ending with zucchini.
7. Pour tomato paste mixture over layered vegetables. Cover with foil; bake for an additional 50 minutes. Remove foil and bake uncovered for another 15 minutes.
